title: Input editor and keybindings
purpose: The user needs to type prompts, submit them, navigate history, and compose multi-line input. This brief builds the input editor that renders in the fixed panel, handles key events, and manages history persistence.
task: Implement the multi-line input editor in input/editor.rs. Enter submits, Shift+Enter (Kitty keyboard) or Alt+Enter (fallback) inserts newlines. The input area grows upward when newlines are added, triggering DECSTBM reissue via the fixed panel compositor. Escape clears input, Ctrl+C on empty exits. Up/Down cycle through history when no autocomplete popup is open. History persists to ~/.norn/history.txt. Implement the key event → action mapping in input/keybindings.rs.

requirements.title: Input editor state and rendering
requirements.spec: The input editor SHALL maintain a multi-line text buffer and render it in the fixed panel's input area.
acceptance: InputEditor struct holds: lines (Vec<String>), cursor_row, cursor_col
render(row, width, writer, caps) writes the input content at the specified fixed panel rows
Cursor is positioned at (cursor_row, cursor_col) within the input area after rendering
height() returns the number of lines (minimum 1)
Test: empty editor has height 1
Test: editor with 3 lines has height 3

requirements.title: Submit on Enter
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Enter, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit the current input buffer contents to the agent loop AND clear the editor.
acceptance: submit() returns the full input text (all lines joined with newlines) and clears the buffer
After submit, height returns to 1 (single empty line)
Empty input is not submitted (Enter on empty buffer is a no-op)
Test: submit on 'hello\nworld' returns 'hello\nworld' and resets to empty

requirements.title: Multi-line input with newline insertion
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Shift+Enter (Kitty keyboard) or Alt+Enter (fallback), THE SYSTEM SHALL insert a newline at the cursor position AND expand the input area upward.
acceptance: Shift+Enter inserts a newline when TerminalCaps.kitty_keyboard is true
Alt+Enter (ESC followed by Enter) inserts a newline on all terminals
New line is inserted at cursor_row, cursor moves to the start of the new line
Input area height increases by 1, triggering DECSTBM reissue through the fixed panel compositor
Test: inserting a newline on a 1-line editor produces a 2-line editor with height 2

requirements.title: Escape clears input
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Escape, THE SYSTEM SHALL clear the input buffer and reset to a single empty line.
acceptance: Escape clears all lines, resets to single empty line
Cursor resets to (0, 0)
Input area height returns to 1, triggering DECSTBM reissue if it was multi-line
Test: Escape on multi-line input produces empty single-line editor

requirements.title: Ctrl+C exit on empty input
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Ctrl+C AND the input buffer is empty, THE SYSTEM SHALL signal TUI exit. WHEN the input buffer is not empty, Ctrl+C SHALL clear the input (same as Escape).
acceptance: Ctrl+C on empty input returns an Exit action
Ctrl+C on non-empty input clears the buffer (same behaviour as Escape)
Test: Ctrl+C on empty returns Exit action
Test: Ctrl+C on non-empty clears and returns no action

requirements.title: History navigation
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Up/Down arrows AND no autocomplete popup is open, THE SYSTEM SHALL cycle through previous inputs.
acceptance: InputHistory struct holds a Vec<String> of previous submissions and a navigation index
Up arrow replaces the current editor content with the previous history entry
Down arrow moves forward through history; at the end, restores the draft input
The current draft (unsaved input before navigating) is preserved and restored when navigating past the newest entry
Test: submitting 'a' then 'b', then pressing Up twice shows 'a'

requirements.title: History persistence
requirements.spec: Input history SHALL persist to ~/.norn/history.txt, loading on startup and appending on each submission.
acceptance: On startup, load history from ~/.norn/history.txt (one entry per line, most recent last)
On each submit, append the submitted text as a new line
File is created on first submission if it does not exist
File read failure on startup is not fatal — start with empty history
Test: round-trip: save entries, reload, verify order

requirements.title: Key event to action mapping
requirements.spec: The keybindings module SHALL map terminal key events to InputAction variants that the editor and event loop consume.
acceptance: InputAction enum: Submit, InsertNewline, InsertChar(char), Backspace, Delete, CursorLeft, CursorRight, CursorUp, CursorDown, ClearInput, Exit, HistoryPrev, HistoryNext
map_key_event(event, caps, popup_open) -> Option<InputAction> translates terminal events to actions
Shift+Enter maps to InsertNewline when kitty_keyboard is true
Alt+Enter (ESC + Enter sequence) maps to InsertNewline always
Up/Down map to HistoryPrev/HistoryNext when popup_open is false
Test: Enter key maps to Submit
Test: Alt+Enter maps to InsertNewline

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement autocomplete trigger detection or popup rendering — that is NT-005
SHALL NOT implement ProviderEvent handling or agent loop dispatch — that is NT-011
SHALL NOT implement the main event loop — that is NT-011; this brief builds the input components that the event loop consumes
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: Editor submit returns correct text and resets — verified by test
Multi-line newline insertion increases height — verified by test
Escape clears editor — verified by test
History navigation cycles through entries — verified by test
History persistence round-trips — verified by test
Key mapping produces correct actions — verified by tests
